summary: Command families that sit near architecture policy but must keep behavior behind owned module boundaries instead of accumulating rules in docs or command facades.

entries.rule: Keep the command facade as a compatibility export; keep CLI argument parsing and prompt-template section validation in the owner module.
Keep lifecycle validation, persistence, option parsing, and presentation behind the gap-case module boundary.
Keep simulation orchestration, analysis, recommendations, option parsing, and presentation behind the simulate module boundary.
Keep the command facade as migration orchestration only; raw CLI projection, delegated helper routing, and integrity identifier policy stay in the ci-migrate module.
Keep init orchestration in the command facade and move issue-tracker, minimal-mode, hook-template, and package-manager decisions into the init module.
Keep upgrade command compatibility stable while contract/default migration helpers and CLI projection stay in the upgrade module.
Keep the Project Brain command as a dispatcher; status, query, add, preflight, and stale behavior live in focused project-brain modules.
